title: What are the main stages in monthly payroll process?
content:
The main stages of monthly Payroll Process are:
Master Save
Payroll employees master data and payroll settings are getting saved for that months payroll process
Get Timesheets
Payroll employees approved timesheets are taken for payroll process and timesheet not required employees timesheets auto generated based on shift after checking the leave and business travel. Dummy hours as per shift will be taken for the date after cutoff date and up to payroll end date.
Select Feeds
Lists previously generated payroll feed entries and these entries can be selected to include in payroll process
Initial Payroll Generate for Checking
Generates the employee level payroll for checking based on employee save master data, timesheet and selected feed entries
Payroll Transaction Adjustments
Provides the facility to make manual adjustment for the generated payroll data
Submit Payroll
Provides the facility to Submit or Approve the previously generated payroll and adjustment made payroll for Disbursement
Disbursement Payroll
Provides the facility to disburse the processed payroll through Bank/Cheque/Cash
Non Disbursement Payroll
For non disbursed payroll process actual timesheet has to be processed for the dummy hours taken days and for the difference day’s amount is calculated and saved
Finalization
If there is any difference of disbursed and non disbursed payroll then a feed for next payroll period against each employee will be generated. All process stages of the active period will be completed by this process and next payroll period will automatically set active.

title: How to Set Location-wise Mobile Attendance Punch Restrictions?
content:
How to Set Location wise Mobile Attendance Punch Restrictions?
Navigate to the 'Configure Attendance Location Restrictions' page within the 'Settings' section.
Enable location-based punch restrictions by checking the 'Enable location-based punch restriction' checkbox.
Specify the allowable proximity in meters for attendance punches by entering values in the 'How Close Must Employees Be to the Scheduled Location?' field. Punches recorded beyond this specified range will be flagged as outside the acceptable distance.
Optionally, to prevent punches from locations outside the specified range, select the 'Prevent punches from outside scheduled location' checkbox. This ensures that punches recorded beyond the designated range will not be accepted.
Define punch locations on the same page under the 'Locations' section by providing the location address, latitude, and longitude. Set the acceptable range for punches at each location in meters. Note that if a distance of 0 meters is specified, the distance from the 'Settings' section will be used for validation.
Assign punch restrictions to employees based on projects or individual employees. Under the 'Location Mapping' tab on the same page:
For project-wise restrictions, select the 'Type' as 'Project' from the dropdown menu. Choose the relevant project from the 'Assigned To' dropdown. Use the checkboxes to select applicable punch locations for the chosen project. This automatically applies restrictions to employees associated with the mapped project.
For individual employee restrictions, select the 'Type' as 'Employee' from the dropdown. Choose the desired employee details from the 'Assigned To' dropdown. Use the checkboxes next to each listed location to select applicable ones for the selected employee.
Once these settings are configured, the mobile attendance punch system will enforce the specified restrictions based

title: What is Disburse and Non Disburse process in payroll?
content:
Some companies pay the Payroll Salary at the end of the month before receiving the actual timesheets of employees from payroll start date to payroll end date. So Dummy Timesheets based on shift hours are taken during payroll process for the date after cutoff date up to end date.
To handle this there are two stages in payroll, they are Payroll Disbursement and Payroll Non Disbursement.
Disburse Payroll Process
In Disbursed payroll process stage dummy hours will be taken for the date after cutoff date and up to payroll end date. And actual timesheet entered are taken between the payroll period start date to cut off date.
Non Disbursed Payroll Process
Before Non Disbursed payroll process actual timesheet has to be entered or processed for the dummy hours taken days. And during Non Disbursed Payroll process stage it compares the dummy hours taken timesheets with actual and for the difference day’s amount will be calculated.
Finalization Payroll Process
During Finalization Process stage the difference amount is forwarded as Previous Month feed in next payroll period.
If Payroll End Date and Cut Off Date are different then the Non disbursed Payroll process stage should run only after the Payroll End Date, so the actual timesheet should be entered (if timesheet required) for the days between cutoff date and payroll end date.
If Cut Off date and Payroll End Date are same then no difference feed generated in Non Disbursement Payroll stage, during Disburse Payroll stage itself all days actual timesheets will be taken for payroll process.

Personnel Administration → Employee Probation Confirmation
Based on the selected criteria, a list of employees with pending probation confirmation will be displayed. For each employee listed, the following actions are available:
Regularization
: Confirms the employee’s probation.
Extension
: Extends the probation period.
Note: The "Extension Days" field is mandatory. Remarks can be added if necessary.
Applicable for Separation
: Initiates the separation process for the employee.
System Behaviour:
When an employee is regularized, the
‘Is Under Probation Period’
flag on the Personnel Information page will be updated automatically.
When probation is extended, the
‘End of Probation Period’
date will be updated accordingly.
Employees with extended probation will appear in future search results for confirmation.
To search for employees pending probation confirmation, navigate to:
Personnel Administration → Search Probation Confirmation Employee
To view detailed probation extension history for a specific employee, go to:
Personnel Administration → Personnel Information
Click the
View Probation Extension Details
icon to see the full extension record.
Click below link to view create Employee Probation Confirmation Video:

title: How to Manage Attributes?
content:
The
Manage Attributes
feature allows HR administrators to create or update compensation-related attributes within the system. These attributes may include fixed or variable components that are used for payroll, benefits, or reporting purposes.
Navigate to Manage Attributes:
Go to
Personnel Administration
-
> Profile Management
-
> Manage Attributes.
Enter Attribute Details:
Attribute Code
– Unique identifier for the attribute.
Attribute Category
– Select the relevant category (e.g., Earnings, Deductions, etc.).
Eligibility Group
– Choose the group eligible for this attribute.
Attribute Name
– Enter a clear, descriptive name.
Description
– (Optional) Provide a brief description of the attribute.
Attribute Display Group
– Select where the attribute should appear in reports or profiles.
Define Attribute Type and Values:
Choose one of the attribute types:
Fixed
Advanced
Lump Sum
Enter the Minimum Amount
Enter the Maximum Amount
Set Applicability:
Select the Applicable Category
Select the appropriate Attribute Payment type
Save the Attribute:
After filling in all required details, click Save to finalize the attribute.

title: How to create or Manage Organization in the system?
content:
Creating organizations or managing the existing organizations can be done through the Manage Organization page available under Personnel Administration module.
Personnel Administration ->  Organization Management -> Manage Organization
Enter the Organization Name, Category like internal organization or external organization, Organization level (here HR Organization means the organization where employees exists) and other required details to create the organization record. Click the save button to save the data.
Once the organization is created it has to be attached to the organization tree as a node. This can be done through the Manage Organization Hierarchy page view option.
Personnel Administration ->  Organization Management -> Manage Organization Hierarchy
Select organization from the left Organization Hierarchy section under which the new organization to be added and select & move the organization units from right section to left. Click the Save button to save the changes.

title: How is employee data updated in bulk using the Bulk Employee Data Update feature?
content:
The
Bulk Employee Data Update
feature allows users to apply specific changes to multiple employees at once. This functionality helps HR teams efficiently manage updates to employee attributes such as holiday calendar, payroll approval settings, overtime eligibility, and more.
Navigation Path
:
Personnel Administration -> Bulk Employee Data Update
Follow the steps below to update employee data in bulk:
1. Search for Employees
Use the available filters to search for employees:
Organization Unit (OU)
Employee Name/ID
Field Type (e.g., Holiday Calendar, Approval for Payroll, Eligible for Overtime, etc.)
Field Value (current value if applicable)
Click
Search
to display matching employees.
2. Select Employees and Apply Changes
Select one or more employees from the search results.
Choose the Field Type you want to update (e.g., Holiday Calendar).
Enter or select the new Field Value.
Click
Save
to apply the changes to all selected employees.
This tool enables centralized updates, reduces manual errors, and improves administrative efficiency in managing employee records. Users can view the updated information in
Personnel Administration → Personnel Information
.

title: How to Manage Chart Properties?
content:
The
Manage Chart Properties
feature allows administrators to define specific properties for each chart type used in the dashboard. These properties help control the appearance and behavior of charts, enabling more customized and dynamic visualizations.
Navigation Path:
System Admin → Dashboard Settings → Manage Chart Properties
Steps to Manage Chart Properties:
Select Chart Type- Choose the appropriate chart type (e.g., Bar, Pie, Line) from the available options. This determines which chart the property will apply to.
Enter Property Name- Specify the name of the property. This should be a unique and meaningful identifier that reflects its purpose.
Enter Description- Provide a brief description explaining the property's role or effect on the chart's behavior or layout.
Click Save- After entering the required details, click Save to store the chart property. It will now be available for use with the selected chart type.

title: How to set up a new attendance device in the Attendance module?
content:
The Device Setup option under the Attendance module is used to configure biometric or other attendance tracking devices.
How to Set Up a Device:
Navigate to:
Attendance → Settings → Device Setup
Enter the Required Details:
Select Location (where the device is installed)
Device Name
Device ID
Floor (optional, if applicable)
Type – Specify whether the device is for IN or OUT
Check the Device for Attendance option to enable it for attendance tracking
Save the Configuration:
Click Save to complete the device setup.

title: How to publish existing reports or modified reports in the system?
content:
Manage RDLC report page is used to manage all published RDLC reports in the application or download and publish the modified reports.
System Admin-> Manage RDLC Reports -> Manage RDLC report
Following are the options available under this menu.
Edit existing reports
Delete existing reports
Download and modify existing reports
Upload modified reports
Publish new reports
You can download existing reports RDLC file and can do changes as per your requirements. Once changes were done, then can again upload and overwrite the existing report file.
Publish new reports option is for the application developer to create and publish new reports, the report file will get published and saved in reports forlder automatically. This option is not for the admin or user.
Click below link to view create Manage RDLC report Video:

title: How to do a Manual Advance Request and manage the request without workflow?
content:
Advance Request can be managed without workflow through the 'Advance Request' page under Personnel Administration module.
Personnel Administration -> Advance Request -> Advance Request
Any type of Advance request can be processed through the system. Advance can be requested for self or any permitted employee. The already requested details can be searched by ‘Requested For’ or by ‘Reference No’. The requested for employee details and pending advance dashboard are displayed below the search option.
The Reference No is automatically generated. Maximum eligibility for the selected Advance type is displayed based on the settings given. Advance amount can split by ‘No of Installments’ or ‘Installment Amount’ by selecting the ‘Installments Split By’ dropdown. Depends on the selection the Installments or Installment Amount field will be enabled for entry. Deduction Start can be selected and the Deduction End is calculated as deduction start + no of installments and a valid payroll period validation given (already opened payroll period).
The Installment breakup shows the deduction in each payroll period details and the current status of each deduction.
Advance Requested Amount will be generated as ERP Payment entry and this can be issued thru the ‘Pay Through ERP’ page under Payroll module. And the corresponding payroll deduction entries also generated for payroll process. Validation is set against the advance type settings.
The requested employees ESB details can be viewed by clicking the ‘ESB Calculation’ button, it will popup the ESB page with employee details.
Advance Request List will list all the request details of the selected employee. If any of the deductions were processed in payroll then the Edit and Delete options will be disabled for that request. And workflow requests also cannot delete from manual page.
How to extend the installment deduction payroll period?
The deduction can be extended by clicking the Extend icon from the grid. Extending a deduction can be done by selecting the pending deduction and selecting the Extending Period. Remarks can be given for the extend record.
If the ‘Extend in between installments’ is selected then all the deductions in between the actual deduction and Extending Period will also get extended.
How to do the settlement of pending deduction amounts?
Entire pending deductions can be settled by clicking the Settlements icon from the grid. On settlement selection the entire pending amount will be cumulated and shown as Pending amount and it is read only. The settlement can be done in one installment or multiple but should be less than current pending installments.
The settled deduction entries will be generated for payroll process.
How to do the Special settlement of pending deduction amounts during payroll process?
This option is to settle the pending advance/loan amount to current payroll period. This option required to do when the payroll is in-progress, means when the generate payroll process stage completed and the submit payroll process is not yet completed. This can be done by clicking the Special Settlements icon from the grid. This option will be enabled for permitted role users, the permission can be set in advance type master page. On Special settlement selection the entire pending amount will be cumulated and shown as Pending amount and it is read only. The settlement can be done in one installment or multiple but should be less than current pending installments.
The settled deduction entries will be generated for payroll process.

title: How to manage asset items or asset item status?
content:
Non allocated asset items details or status can be edited through the 'Manage Asset Item Details' page.
Search items by selecting the required selection criteria. From the search result grid edit the item and do the required modification. When the items are selected from the search result, the 'Change Item Status Detail' section will be displayed to change the status of the selected items.
Non allocated asset items status can also be changed through the 'Print Asset Receipt/Barcode' page by clicking the 'Manage Item Status' icon.

title: How to Create New Survey
content:
The
Create New Survey
feature allows administrators to create surveys for collecting feedback, conducting assessments, or gathering employee opinions.
1. Access the Survey Creation Page
Navigate to:
Admin Support Functions
-
> Survey
-
> Create New Survey
2. Enter Survey Details
Name: Provide a title for the survey.
Description: Enter a brief description outlining the purpose of the survey.
3.  Create from Existing Survey(Optional)
If you wish to base the new survey on an existing one, tick the Create From Existing checkbox.
This will allow you to copy the structure and content of a previously created survey.
4. Save the Survey
Click Save to create the survey and proceed to add questions or make further
customization
.

title: How to Manage Error Logs
content:
The
Manage Error Logs
feature allows system administrators to track and review system-related errors or user activity issues. This tool helps in identifying and resolving technical problems efficiently.
1. Access the Page
Navigate to:
System Admin
-
> Manage Error Logs
2. Enter Search Criteria
Use the following filters to locate specific error logs:
Error Category:
Select the type of error (e.g., System Error, Validation Error, Permission Error).
Employee:
Enter the Employee ID to filter logs for a specific user.
Employee Name:
Optionally enter the name to narrow down the search.
Module Name:
Specify the system module where the error occurred (e.g., Asset Management, Payroll).
Period:
Define the date range during which the error occurred.
3. View Results
Click View to display the matching error logs.
The results include details such as:
Error message

title: How to create or manage Leave Type?
content:
The
Manage Leave Type
page allows administrators to define and configure various leave types based on organizational policies. Leave types can be customized to apply to specific employee groups based on gender, marital status, or religion.
Accessing Leave Type Settings
Navigate to:
Leave Management -> Settings -> Manage Leave Type
Enter Leave Type Details
Code – Unique identifier for the leave type
Name – Name of the leave type (e.g., Annual Leave, Sick Leave)
Description – A brief explanation of the leave type
Set Eligibility Criteria
Applicable Gender – Select the gender(s) eligible for this leave (e.g., Male, Female, All)
Applicable Marital Status – Choose the applicable marital status (e.g., Single, Married)
Applicable Religion – Select the religions this leave type applies to (if applicable)
Save the Leave Type
Click Save to add the new leave type to the system.
Once created, the leave type will be available for use in various HR and leave-related functionalities.

title: Payroll Dashboard
content:
The
Payroll Dashboard
offers a
comprehensive and visual summary
of monthly payroll activities, providing valuable insights into employee movement, salary distribution, and payroll trends. It supports HR and payroll teams in tracking payroll operations efficiently and accurately.
Navigation Path:
Dashboard
→
Payroll Dashboard
Purpose:
To present key payroll metrics and trends through a centralized and interactive dashboard, helping decision-makers stay informed and manage payroll processes effectively.
Key Features:
Monthly Overview
Displays summary information for the selected payroll month:
Number of Employees
New Joinees
Separated Employees
Total Payroll Amount
Payroll Breakdown
Provides detailed visual representation of:
Allowances – Component-wise breakdown (e.g., basic salary, HRA, transport).
Deductions – Statutory and non-statutory deductions (e.g., tax, loans, penalties).
Additional Insights
Comparative data across previous months
Highlighted payroll exceptions or adjustments
Graphs showing trends in salary components and employee movements
Benefits:
Enables quick understanding of monthly payroll outcomes.
Enhances data-driven decision-making for HR and finance teams.
Identifies significant employee changes and financial impact.
Promotes transparency in payroll management.

title: When the newly modified Overtime factors will get effective in timesheet and payroll?
content: Overtime factors will get saved against the timesheet when it is generated, So after generating the timesheet if the Overtime factor is changed then that will not get affected in that generated timesheet, In that case delete the generated timesheet and recreate it to apply the new overtime factors.

title: How to Manage Item Receipt
content:
The
Manage Item Receipt
feature allows users to confirm the receipt of items associated with a document transfer. This ensures proper tracking and acknowledgment of all items received.
1. Access the Manage Item Receipt Page
Navigate to:
Admin Support Functions -> Document Dispatch -> Manage Item Receipt
2. Search for the Document Transfer
Use the available search filters to locate the document transfer by entering the following details:
Transfer Type
Transfer From (Employee Name)
Transfer To (Employee Name)
Courier Reference
Deliver To
Received By
Period (Start Date and End Date)
Click Search to display the relevant transfer records.
3. Select and Confirm Items
From the search results, select the desired Document Transfer.
In the Item List section, select the item(s) you have received.
Enter the Received By name.
Enter the Received Date.
Click Save to confirm the receipt of the selected item(s).

title: How to manage GR Payments
content:
The
Manage GR Payments
page under the Government Relations module allows users to view and track government-related payment transactions.
Navigation Path:
Government Relations → Manage GR Payments
Purpose:
To search and review government payment records, such as visa charges, license fees, or other statutory payments made on behalf of employees or the company.
Search Filters:
Company
– Select the relevant company
Received Status
– Filter based on whether the payment has been received or not
Charged For
– Specify the service or reason for the charge (e.g., Visa, Iqama, License)

title: How is the Overseas Joining Process managed through the workflow after hiring an international (overseas) candidate?
content:
The
Overseas Workflow
is automatically triggered when hiring an international (overseas) candidate. It guides HR through the essential steps of visa processing, onboarding, and employee data setup tailored to overseas hires.
Accessing the Workflow
To access the Overseas Joining Process workflow:
Navigate to:
Workflow → Worklist
Locate the request labelled:
Overseas Joining Process
(Automatically generated upon hiring an overseas candidate.)
1. Visa Allocation
In this task, the HR user must choose one of the following options:
Work Visa Available
Bring Candidate on Visit Visa
Click Submit to initiate this task.
2. Visit Visa Process
If Visit Visa is selected, complete the following fields in the Visit Visa Process task:
Current Employer Name
Profession
Visa Type
Visa Order Number
Visa Number
Approval Date
Visa Duration
Processing Agent (select from available list)
Click Submit to complete the task.
3. Complete Onboarding
Enter the Departure and Arrival Airport Details for the candidate.
Click Submit to complete the task.
4. Update Employee Information
Fill in all relevant employee details:
Joining Date
Employee ID
Visa and Residency Data
Contact Information
Emergency Contact Details
Initiating Work Visa Process (If Required)
Tick the
"Initiate Work Visa Process"
checkbox if the employer chooses to proceed with a work visa.
If Yes is selected, the system automatically triggers the Work Visa Processing Workflow.
Click Submit to complete the workflow.
Once the
Overseas Joining Process
workflow is completed, the employee’s data will be available in the system.
To view the onboarded employee’s information:
Navigate to:
Personnel Administration → Personnel Information
The system will display:
Personal Details
Employment Records
Visa and Contractual Information

title: Where can the Pending Review List be found?
content:
The
Pending Review List
, available under the
Performance Evaluation
module, allows users to track performance appraisals that are still pending review or submission. This helps ensure timely follow-up and the successful completion of appraisal tasks.
1.
Navigate to the Pending Review Section
Go to:
Performance Evaluation → Pending Review List
2.
Enter Search Criteria
Fill in any of the following fields to filter the results:
Year
Employee Name
Company
Click the Search button to display the results.
3. View Pending Appraisals
The system will show a list of pending performance appraisal tasks based on the entered criteria.
You can view individual pending items and take necessary follow-up actions.

title: How to cancel leave through the workflow?
content:
The
Leave Cancellation
feature allows employees or HR users to cancel previously submitted leave requests, provided that the
Rejoin task has not been initiated
.
Canceling
a leave request helps restore the associated leave eligibility for future use.
Navigation Path
:
Workflow → New Request → Leave Cancellation
Important Notes
Only leave requests where the ‘Employee Rejoin Confirmation – Requestor’ task is not submitted are eligible for cancellation.
Once the Rejoin process has started, leave cancellation is not permitted.
Search for Eligible Leave Requests
Use filters such as:
Employee Name
Process ID
Locate pending leave rejoin records eligible for cancellation.
Select the Leave Entry
From the search results, choose the leave request to be
canceled
.
Enter Cancellation Remarks
Provide a valid reason for
canceling
the leave in the Remarks field.
Add to List
Click the Add button to include the selected leave entry in the cancellation list.
Submit the Request
Click Submit to initiate and process the Leave Cancellation workflow
.
Successfully
canceled
leave requests will
automatically restore
the applicable leave eligibility to the employee’s leave balance.

title: How is a vehicle color added in the system?

The
Vehicle Colour
setting allows administrators to maintain a predefined list of vehicle colours. This ensures consistency in data entry and improves the accuracy of vehicle records and reporting
.
Navigation Path:
Admin Support Functions → Vehicle Management → Settings → Vehicle Colour
Navigate to the Vehicle Colour page using the path above.
Enter the following details:
Code – A short code representing the colour (e.g.,
BLK
for Black,
WHT
for White).
Description – The full name of the colour (e.g.,
Black
,
White
).
Click ‘Save’ to add the colour to the system.
The saved vehicle colours will be available for selection on the Vehicle Master page during vehicle registration or updates
.

title: How to add Ticket/Exit Re-Entry (ERE) Eligibility?
content:
This feature allows HR users to manage
Ticket
and
Exit Re-Entry Visa
eligibility records for employees and their dependents. It ensures accurate tracking and administration of contractual entitlements based on the employee’s contract terms.
Navigation Path
Personnel Administration → Ticket / Exit Re-Entry → Ticket / Exit Re-Entry Eligibility
Select the Employee
Choose the required employee from the list.
The system automatically displays the employee's and dependents’ information based on
the values defined in the employee’s contract:
Navigation Path: Personnel Administration → Personnel Information → Contract and Benefits
Number of Tickets in Contract
Number of Exit Re-Entry Visas in Contract
These values determine the available entitlements and control how eligibility records are managed and displayed on the Ticket / Exit Re-Entry Eligibility page.
Add Eligibility
From the Eligibility Option, select whether the entry is for the
Employee or a Dependent.
Click Add Eligibility to enter new eligibility details.
Search and Manage Eligibility
Search for existing eligibility entries using:
Status
Eligibility Type (Ticket / Exit Re-Entry)
From the results list, select an entry to perform one of the following actions:
Mark Eligible
– Update the status to eligible.
Mark Utilized
– Indicate the eligibility has been used.
Mark Lapsed
– Mark the entry as expired or unused.
Delete
– Remove the selected eligibility entry.

title: How to request any Card thru workflow?
content:
New Work Flow details can be entered thru the 'Card Request' page under Work Flow module.User can request for any type of defined Cards (like Business Card, ID Card, etc) thru this workflow.
New Request -> Card Request
What all details the Requestor has to enter in Card Request?
Requested For  -  Select the Requested For Employee
Card Type   -  Any type of master defined Card Type (like Business Card, ID Card, etc) can be requested by selecting the Card Type
No. of Cards Required   -  By default it comes from the master data defined and user can modify
Name (English and Arabic)  - This is the Name to be Appeared in the Card, by default the requested for employee name appear here and user can modify
Designation (English and Arabic) - This is the Designation to be Appeared in the Card, by default the requested for employee position appear here and user can modify
Phone, Extension, Mobile and Email - This displayed information will Appear in the Card, by default the requested for employee’s details appear here and user can modify
Who all can do the Card Request?
Employee or Admin can request Card for self or for any permitted employee.
How can the requestor attach the required documents along with the card request?
The attachments can be added in card request thru the common attachment option available in the Action History area.
What are the tasks available in Card Request workflow?
There are many functional and approval tasks available in Card Request. The task list is added in the process view.
What is the role of Approver task owners and Process and Issue task owner?
The Approver task owners have to verify and approve or rework based on the permission.
Process and Issue task owner has to prepare the requested card, issue to the requestor and close the request
What is the Business Rule of Card Request?
The Business Rule describes the Card Request workflow tasks, actions of each task, description of each and the next task owner.

Service Desk Ticket

Service Desk → Create Service Desk Ticket

title: How to create a new Day Definition in the Attendance module?
content:
The
Day Definition
feature under the Attendance module allows users to define working and non-working days for scheduling and attendance purposes.
This configuration helps in accurate scheduling, attendance tracking, and payroll processing.
Navigate to:
Attendance → Settings → Day Definition
Enter the Following Details:
Day Name (e.g., Sunday, Monday)
Abbreviation (e.g., Sun, Mon)
Select Position (defines the order of the day in the week)
Save the Day Definition:

.

title: How to create a new Shift?
content:
This is to create a new shift, for assigning to an employee thru the 'Shift Definition' page under Attendance module.
Attendance ->  Settings ->  Shift Definition
Shift details and the working days in this shift and for each working day normal working hours, Ramadan working hours can be entered.
The shifts created here can be used to assign to employee or used to assign default shift to OU.
Grace Time will not reflect in Late arrival, but if full shift hours are not worked then will appear in Early leave list.
Attendance punches in between Maximum Early In Time and Maximum Late OUT Time only will be considered during attendance import for this shift.
Working days wise Shift timings can be modified to reflect different timings in each day of the week.
Once the Attendance punch is synced for the shift which has been assigned to any employee then the modification of that shift timing is blocked; only name and description can be modified.
If required to have a different shift timing for an active employee then create a new shift and assign the new shift to those employees from required date. Overlapping of multiple shifts is not allowed.

Create the new Project by mapping the Organization thru:
Timesheet->Settings->Manage Projects

title: How to Manage Course
content:
The
Manage Course
page is used to define and manage all training courses offered within the organization. It serves as a centralized repository of available training programs that can be referenced in training plans and employee nominations.
Navigation Path:
Learning & Development → Settings → Manage Course
To add or manage a training course, follow these steps:
Training Type – Select the type of training.
Course Code – Enter a unique code to identify the course.
Course Title – Provide a descriptive title for the course.
Course Details – Enter detailed information or description about the course.
Cost in Base Currency – Specify the training cost in the organization's base currency.
Certification Program (Yes/No) – Indicate whether the course provides certification.
Duration – Mention the course duration (in hours or days).
Recommended As – Choose whether the course is:
General – Applicable to all employees
Position Specific – Relevant to specific roles or positions
Click Save – To save the course details.
This page helps the L&D team maintain a centralized repository of all available training programs, which can be used in training plans and nominations.

title: How to Reject a Mobile Attendance Punch?
content:
How to Reject a Mobile Attendance Punch?
Access the 'Realtime Attendance OU based' page under the Attendance module.
Use the 'Filter' option available on the page to narrow down and locate the required attendance punch.
From the list of attendance punches, select the ones you want to reject by ticking the checkboxes next to them.
Click the 'Reject' button located below the result grid. This action will mark the selected punches as rejected.
Following the rejection, the attendance summary will be recalculated to reflect the changes.

title: Where can I view the count and details of employees who are late, leave early, or are absent?
content:
Where can I view the count and details of employees who are late, leave early, or are absent?
Managers can access this information for their subordinates or employees within their permitted organizations through the 'Attendance 360 Dashboard.' By clicking on the respective counts, detailed information is displayed on a popup page.

title: How to change the next Task Responsible role of the Workflow process?
content:
Manage Dynamic Task can be The next task responsible role of the workflow process can be changed by Admin ' Manage Dynamic Task' page under System Admin module.
System Admin-> Workflow Management -> Settings -> Manage Dynamic Task
The next task responsible role of the workflow process can be changed by Admin, the steps are:
Select the ‘Manage Dynamic Task / Modify Build In Task’ tab page.
Select the Workflow model from list
Edit the responsible role change required task
Click the role selection icon
Select the ‘Roles’ from the list, search the roles and select the role from the grid list. Click Ok button after selection.
Save the changes

title: How to Manage Letter / Certificates?
content:
The
Manage Letter / Certificates
feature allows users to request and manage various employee-related letters and certificates (e.g., salary certificate, employment letter, experience certificate) through the HR application.
Navigation Path:
Personnel Administration → Letter / Certificates → Manage Letter / Certificates
Search for Existing Letters or Certificates
Search Filters:
Employee: Select the employee whose letter/certificate you want to view.
Status: Choose the status of the request (e.g., Approved, Pending, Rejected).
Company: Select the employee’s company.
Letter / Certificate Type: Choose the type of letter or certificate.
Period: Define the time period, if applicable.
2.Click Search to view results.
Request a New Letter or Certificate
Click the New Letter button to initiate a new request.
Enter the Required Details:
Requested For: Select the employee for whom the letter is required.
Company: Choose the applicable company.
Letter Type: Select the letter or certificate type (e.g., Employment Letter, Salary Certificate).

Employee Name As Per: Choose whether the employee's name should appear as per Passport, Visa, or other official record.

3. Save the Request- Click Save to submit the letter/certificate request.
4. View the Letter- Once the letter is generated, click View Letter to preview or download the document.
Employees or authorized users can initiate a Letter and Certificate request through the workflow

title: How to Manage Dashboards?
content:
The
Manage Dashboards
feature enables administrators to create and configure custom dashboards by selecting relevant charts, defining chart properties, and setting display parameters. This allows for flexible and role-based data visualization tailored to specific modules and organizational units.
Navigation Path:
System Admin → Dashboard Settings → Manage Dashboards
Steps to Configure a Dashboard:
Select Module- Choose the relevant module for which the dashboard is being created. This ensures the dashboard is aligned with the module's functionality.
Select Chart Properties- Select one or more chart properties to define how data will be visualized.
Select Chart Type- Choose the desired chart type (e.g., Bar, Pie, Line) that best represents the data.
Select Charts- Assign the charts to be included on the dashboard. You may choose multiple charts to provide a comprehensive view.
Enter Dashboard Name- Provide a unique and descriptive name for the dashboard. This will be used to identify it within the system.
Enter Query- Define the query that will retrieve the data displayed on the dashboard. Ensure the query is optimized and returns the necessary data fields.
OU Permission Required (Optional)- Enable this setting if the dashboard's visibility should be restricted based on Organizational Unit (OU) permissions. This ensures data is accessible only to authorized users.
Click Save- After completing all configurations, click Save to create and store the dashboard.

title: How to redirect alerts or sent the copy of alerts to specified email ids?
content:
Admin can configure email forwarding options in the application through the 'E-mail Settings' page under System Admin module, the steps are:
System Admin -> Global Settings -> E-mail Settings
Section  - Mail
If the option selected is:
Direct - then all the emails from the application will work as it is, no change.
Re-direct - then all the emails from the application will redirect to the id’s specified in To, CC & BCC of this page.
Include - then all the email from the application will also send to the id’s specified in To, CC & BCC of this page.
Section - Comments
If the option selected is:
Include - then whatever text messages given in Subject, Mail Body & Disclaimer of this page will also be added with the emails going out of the application.
Not Include - than all the email from the application will work as it is, nothing will be added

title: How to request an Advance Request thru workflow?
content:
New Work Flow details can be entered thru the 'Advance Request' page under Work Flow module .
New Request -> Advance Request
User can request for advance/loan through the system and the corresponding paying and deducting feeds will be generated for payroll.
What all details the Requestor has to enter in Advance Request?
Requested For  - Select the Employee for Advance Request
Reference No -  System Auto Generated Number
Maximum eligibility as per Settings  -  Maximum eligibility for the selected Advance type is displayed based on the settings given
Advance Type  -  Select the Advance required type like Employee Loan, Salary Advance, etc
Advance Amount  -  Enter Advance Amount required
Installment Split By -  Advance amount can split by ‘No of Installments’ or ‘Installment Amount’ by selecting the ‘Installments Split By’ dropdown
Installments -  No. of installments required; this will be enabled /disabled based on Installment Split By value
Installment Amount - Installment Amount required; this will be enabled /disabled based on Installment Split By value
Deduction Start - Installment deduction will start from the selected payroll period. In the dropdown the payroll periods will be listed for selection. The first payroll period in the dropdown will be the active payroll period, if the Select Feed stage is not completed else next payroll period will be the first payroll period for selection.
Deduction End - Deduction End is calculated as deduction start + no of installments; this is automatically calculated
Remarks - Free text area for any remarks entry
Guarantor - Guarantor Employee selection. This area will be visible only if Guarantor required is marked in the selected advance types’ master settings
In the approval tasks, the requested employees ESB details can be viewed by clicking the ‘ESB Calculation’ button, it will popup the ESB page with employee details. The approver can also view the previous advance request transaction details of the requested for employee and enter the recommendation reason before submission of the task.
Who all can request the Advance Request?
Employee or Admin can request advance for self or for any permitted employee.
How can the requestor attach the required documents along with the Advance Request?
The attachments can be added in advance request thru the common attachment option available in the Action History area.
What are the tasks available in Advance Request workflow?
There are many functional and approval tasks available in advance request. The task list is added in the process view.
When the Advance Amount Payment generated?
On completion of the advance request workflow the ERP Payment entry and the number of installment deduction entries for payroll will be generated.
Who will pay the approved advance to employee?
Advance Requested Amount will be generated as ERP Payment entry and this can be issued thru the ‘Pay Through ERP’ page under Payroll module. And the corresponding payroll installment deduction entries also generated for payroll process.
What is the Business Rule of Advance Request?
The Business Rule describes the advance request workflow tasks, actions of each task, description of each and the next task owner.

title: How Partial Absence hours deduction and Partial Presence hours payment is calculated in different payroll calculation methods?
content:
How Partial Absence hours deduction and Partial presence hours payment is calculated in different payroll calculation methods?
The Partial Absence hours deduction is calculated based on the payroll calculation method selected:
Partial Absence hours deduction rate in case of Fixed Days Fixed Hour Payroll Calculation:
Total Payroll Package for Partial Absent Days / 30 / 8
Partial Absence hours deduction rate in case of Fixed Days Shift Hour Payroll Calculation:
Total Payroll Package for Partial Absent Days / 30 / That Days Shift Hour
Partial Presence hours payment rate is calculated based on Calendar Days Payroll Calculation:
Total Payroll Package for Partial Absent Days / Number of Days in that Payroll Period / That Days Shift Hour
Note: 'Total Payroll Package for Partial Absent Days' Numerators are settings in the applications.
How to set Partial Absence numerators (select benefits and deductions)?
To choose what all packages to be selected for Partial Absence Days calculation can be done thru:
Timesheet Module->Settings->Manage BLS Project Other Than LeavType Numerator Settings

title: How to change the Subordinates of one Immediate Supervisor (Report To) to another Immediate Supervisor (Report To)?
content:
The
Employee Re-assignment / Transfer
feature enables HR administrators to transfer an individual employee or a group of employees from one organizational unit to another within the system. This process is managed through the Employee Re-assignment / Transfer page in the Personnel Administration module.
Navigation Path:

Search for Employees

Select Employees grid under the Source Details section.
Select Employees for Transfer

Define Destination Details
In the Destination Details section, select the target organization unit. Optionally, you may also update or assign:
Location
Shift
Timesheet Project
Reporting To (Supervisor/Manager)
Add to Transfer List

Add
button to move the selected employees to the
Selected Employees
list.
Save Transfer
After verifying the destination details and selected employees, click the
Save
button to submit the transfer for confirmation.
Post-Save Action
The transfer entry will appear in the
Employee Re-assignment History
grid with the status marked as
“Confirmation Pending”
.
HR or other authorized personnel can later confirm the transfer to complete the reassignment process.
After confirmation:
Navigate to the
Personnel Administration->
Personnel Information
page.
Verify the updated organization-related information, which will now reflect the transferred organizational details.
This feature facilitates efficient and traceable employee movements between organizational units, while maintaining a historical record of all transfers for audit and tracking purposes.

title: Is there any facilities to create as many Support Function categories?
content:
Support Functions categories are fixed like ADMIN, HR, IT and FINANCE. It is not possible to create any other Support Function other than this.
User can create any number of Sub Functions under this Support Functions
